code: @Manual{,   title = {pTRAPPING: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R},   author = {Camilo Rodríguez},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/laurenoconnelllab/pTRAPPING}, }

what: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: pTRAPPING R package provides streamlined, reproducible workflow analysing TRAP-seq PhosphoTRAP RNA-sequencing data. wraps statistical power edgeR limma behind compact set purpose-built functions take raw counts matrix publication-ready tables figures just lines code. TRAP-seq? Translating Ribosome Affinity Purification followed sequencing (TRAP-seq) captures mRNAs actively translated specific cell type, tagged transgenic ribosomal subunit. key comparison IP fraction (immunoprecipitated, actively translated transcripts) INPUT fraction (total RNA — transcripts). Genes significantly enriched IP relative INPUT translated tagged cell type. PhosphoTRAP adds activity-marking phosphorylation step, recently activated neurons captured — combining cell-type specificity behavioural state information single experiment.

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Throughout README simulate mouse TRAP-seq experiment studying pair-bonding preoptic area (POA) hypothalamus — brain region well-known role social behaviour. Mice divided two groups: nb – non-bonded control mice pb – pair-bonded mice (48 h co-housing partner) group 3 animals (biological replicates). animal contributes one IP one INPUT library, giving 12 samples total. column-naming convention <treatment><replicate><fraction> (e.g. nb1INPUT, pb2IP) lets ptrap_de() parse sample metadata automatically — sample_df needed. first column holds gene identifiers; remaining 12 columns raw counts. Snap25 (neuronal) already shows high IP counts groups, Oxtr (pair-bonding marker) shows balanced IP/INPUT — enriched pb.
code: library(pTRAPPING) library(ggplot2)  set.seed(42)  n_genes  <- 2000 gene_ids <- paste0("Gene", seq_len(n_genes))  # ── Replace early positions with real mouse gene names ────────────────────── # Neuronal markers — expected to be enriched in IP in BOTH groups neuronal   <- c("Snap25", "Rbfox3", "Camk2a", "Vglut1", "Syn1",                 "Map2",   "Nrgn",   "Bdnf",   "Pvalb",  "Sst") # Pair-bonding markers — enriched in IP only in pb pb_markers <- c("Oxtr", "Avpr1a", "Fos", "Arc", "Egr1") # Glial markers — NOT enriched in neuronal IP (control transcripts) glial      <- c("Gfap", "Aldh1l1", "S100b", "Cx3cr1", "Tmem119")  gene_ids[seq_along(neuronal)]                                       <- neuronal gene_ids[length(neuronal) + seq_along(pb_markers)]                  <- pb_markers gene_ids[length(neuronal) + length(pb_markers) + seq_along(glial)]  <- glial  # ── True IP / INPUT enrichment ratios ─────────────────────────────────────── base_enrich        <- rep(1.0, n_genes) base_enrich[1:10]  <- 8   # neuronal markers — constitutively enriched in both groups base_enrich[11:15] <- 1   # pair-bonding markers — baseline (no enrichment in nb)  pb_extra           <- rep(1.0, n_genes) pb_extra[11:15]    <- 8   # pair-bonding markers strongly enriched only in pb  # ── Helper: simulate one IP/INPUT pair for all genes ──────────────────────── sim_pair <- function(enrich, mu = 200, size = 10, sd_noise = 0.15) {   input <- rnbinom(n_genes, mu = mu, size = size)   ip    <- pmax(round(input * enrich * exp(rnorm(n_genes, 0, sd_noise))), 0L)   list(input = input, ip = ip) }  # ── Build counts data frame with auto-parseable column names ──────────────── cols <- lapply(1:3, function(i) {   nb <- sim_pair(base_enrich)   pb <- sim_pair(base_enrich * pb_extra)   setNames(     data.frame(nb$input, nb$ip, pb$input, pb$ip),     c(paste0("nb", i, "INPUT"), paste0("nb", i, "IP"),       paste0("pb", i, "INPUT"), paste0("pb", i, "IP"))   ) })  counts_mat <- data.frame(Gene = gene_ids, do.call(cbind, cols))  # Preview — neuronal marker, pair-bonding marker, glial marker counts_mat[c(1, 11, 16), 1:7] #>      Gene nb1INPUT nb1IP pb1INPUT pb1IP nb2INPUT nb2IP #> 1  Snap25      274  2231      150   910      229  1467 #> 11   Oxtr      217   260       83   630      290   333 #> 16   Gfap      151   117      143   123      222   307

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: sample_df omitted, ptrap_de() reads treatment, replicate number, fraction directly column names. Providing treatment_name tells function group analyse: neuronal markers (Snap25, Camk2a, etc.) enriched IP groups. pair-bonding markers (Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1) appear pb result — exactly expected activity-dependent transcripts pair-bonded neurons.
code: res_nb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "LRT"        # edgeR likelihood ratio test ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes in nb head(res_nb[res_nb$diffexpressed != "NO",             c("Gene", "logFC", "LR", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 x 6 #>   Gene   logFC    LR    PValue       FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Sst     2.95  695. 3.27e-153 6.54e-150 UP            #> 2 Syn1    3.00  693. 1.10e-152 1.10e-149 UP            #> 3 Snap25  2.90  656. 1.40e-144 9.31e-142 UP            #> 4 Vglut1  3.01  631. 3.12e-139 1.56e-136 UP            #> 5 Bdnf    2.92  624. 1.13e-137 4.50e-135 UP            #> 6 Rbfox3  2.79  616. 5.50e-136 1.83e-133 UP res_pb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes in pb head(res_pb[res_pb$diffexpressed != "NO",             c("Gene", "logFC", "LR", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 x 6 #>   Gene  logFC    LR    PValue       FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Egr1   3.23  629. 6.88e-139 1.38e-135 UP            #> 2 Map2   3.12  600. 1.63e-132 1.63e-129 UP            #> 3 Arc    3.13  591. 1.60e-130 1.07e-127 UP            #> 4 Syn1   2.98  550. 1.36e-121 6.78e-119 UP            #> 5 Sst    2.94  543. 4.18e-120 1.67e-117 UP            #> 6 Nrgn   2.99  542. 5.61e-120 1.87e-117 UP

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Note "paired.ttest": GLM-based methods ("LRT" / "QLF") estimate gene-wise dispersion data work best ≥ 4 replicates. n = 3, dispersion estimates unreliable. paired t-test follows Tan et al. (2016) exactly: “p value gene calculated paired t test input immunoprecipitated RPKM values three experimental repeats.” Set norm.method = "RPKM" (supply gene.length) reproduce original analysis. logFC log₂(mean_IP / mean_INPUT).
code: # Quasi-likelihood F-test — more conservative than LRT res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # limma-voom — accounts for mean-variance trend with precision weights res_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "voom" )  # Paired t-test — recommended for real PhosphoTRAP data with n = 3 animals # Follows the approach in Tan et al. 2016 (Cell 167, 47-59) res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest" )

what: Long-format paired data with return_long = TRUE
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: return_long = TRUE ("paired.ttest") returns per-gene, per-animal paired table used internally — useful plotting individual data points performing additional quality checks:
code: res_long <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # DE results tibble head(res_long$results[, c("Gene", "logFC", "t_statistic", "PValue", "FDR")]) #> # A tibble: 6 x 5 #>   Gene      logFC t_statistic   PValue   FDR #>   <chr>     <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Gene1833  0.140        65   0.000237 0.473 #> 2 Pvalb     2.90         39.4 0.000645 0.645 #> 3 Gene1001 -0.180       -27.6 0.00131  0.772 #> 4 Gene68    0.125        24.5 0.00166  0.772 #> 5 Gene248   0.208        19.7 0.00257  0.772 #> 6 Gene1529  0.194        19.0 0.00275  0.772  # Per-gene, per-animal paired table (showing Snap25): # ip_count / input_count are in the norm.method scale; FE = IP/INPUT per animal res_long$long_data[res_long$long_data$Gene == "Snap25", ] #> # A tibble: 3 x 5 #>   Gene   block ip_count input_count    FE #>   <chr>  <chr>    <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Snap25 1         2231         274  8.12 #> 2 Snap25 2         1467         229  6.38 #> 3 Snap25 3         1398         177  7.86

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Adjust lfc_threshold fdr_threshold control strict DE classification :
code: # Relaxed: any enrichment above 0.5 log2FC at FDR < 0.10 res_relaxed <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT",   lfc_threshold  = 0.5,   fdr_threshold  = 0.10 ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  table(res_relaxed$diffexpressed) #>  #>   NO   UP  #> 1984   16

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Set kable.= TRUE get formatted HTML table top genes — ideal Quarto / R Markdown reports:
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT",   kable.out      = TRUE,   ngenes.out     = 10 )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: classic volcano plot one treatment condition, significant genes colour-coded labelled: plot chunk volcano-nb Customise colours thresholds: plot chunk volcano-pb Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1 appear pb plot — consistent known roles pair-bond formation neuronal immediate-early gene expression.
code: ptrap_volcano(   res_nb,   title = "Non-bonded (nb) — POA" ) ptrap_volcano(   res_pb,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   colors        = c("UP" = "#E69F00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9"),   title         = "Pair-bonded (pb) — POA" )

what: ptrap_volcano2() — dual scatter comparing two treatments
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: ptrap_volcano2() overlays treatment conditions single scatter plot. axis shows log₂ fold-change (IP / INPUT) one group, genes colour-coded DE status across conditions: plot chunk volcano2-default read plot * Top-right quadrant: enriched IP groups → constitutive neuronal translation (e.g. Snap25, Camk2a, Rbfox3). * Bottom-right: enriched pb → activity-dependent translation pair-bonded animals (Oxtr, Fos, Arc, Egr1, Avpr1a). * Top-left: enriched nb → non-bonded-specific. * dotted diagonal identity line — equal enrichment groups. Customise colours thresholds: plot chunk volcano2-custom
code: ptrap_volcano2(   res_nb, res_pb,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   title         = "POA — nb vs pb" ) ptrap_volcano2(   res_nb, res_pb,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   title         = "POA — nb vs pb",   colors        = c(     "DE in both"   = "#D55E00",     "DE only nb"   = "#0072B2",     "DE only pb"   = "#009E73"   ) )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: "voom" applies limma-voom pipeline: counts converted log-CPM precision weights estimated mean-variance trend fitting IP vs INPUT linear model. particularly useful library sizes vary substantially across samples, weights -weight unreliable observations automatically. output structure "LRT" / "QLF" results (Gene, logFC, FDR, diffexpressed, …), drops straight ptrap_volcano(): plot chunk volcano-voom
code: res_nb_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "voom" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes head(res_nb_voom[res_nb_voom$diffexpressed != "NO",                  c("Gene", "logFC", "t", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 x 6 #>   Gene   logFC     t   PValue      FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Sst     2.95  28.9 1.31e-55 1.85e-52 UP            #> 2 Syn1    3.00  28.8 1.85e-55 1.85e-52 UP            #> 3 Vglut1  3.02  27.8 6.39e-54 4.26e-51 UP            #> 4 Snap25  2.90  27.6 1.57e-53 7.84e-51 UP            #> 5 Bdnf    2.91  27.2 5.11e-53 2.04e-50 UP            #> 6 Map2    2.96  27.1 7.69e-53 2.56e-50 UP ptrap_volcano(   res_nb_voom,   title = "Non-bonded (nb) — POA [voom]" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: unpaired t-test method follows PhosphoTRAP approach Knight et al. (2012): per-gene fold enrichments (FE = IP / INPUT) computed every animal group, Welch t-test used compare log₂(FE) two treatment groups. right choice want ask: “IP enrichment different group group B?” rather testing IP vs INPUT within single group. neither treatment_name control_name supplied counts matrix contains exactly two groups, assigned automatically (alphabetically; first = control, second = treatment): Interpretation: diff_FE = mean_FE_pb / mean_FE_nb — ratio mean IP/INPUT fold enrichments treatment control. logFC log2(diff_FE). Genes logFC > 0 enriched pair-bonded group. Pair-bonding markers (Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1) show genes whose neuronal IP enrichment higher pb nb: plot chunk volcano-unpaired
code: # Auto-assigns: nb = control, pb = treatment res_unpaired <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat  = counts_mat,   test_method = "unpaired.ttest" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP #> Auto-assigned treatments alphabetically: control = 'nb', treatment = 'pb'.  # Results include mean FE per group and their ratio (diff_FE) head(res_unpaired[res_unpaired$diffexpressed != "NO",                   c("Gene", "logFC", "diff_FE",                     "mean_FE_nb", "mean_FE_pb",                     "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 4 x 8 #>   Gene  logFC diff_FE mean_FE_nb mean_FE_pb    PValue    FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr> <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Oxtr   2.89    7.40      1.12        8.28 0.0000182 0.0230 UP            #> 2 Egr1   3.24    9.46      1.00        9.50 0.0000376 0.0230 UP            #> 3 Arc    3.14    8.79      1.000       8.79 0.0000392 0.0230 UP            #> 4 Fos    2.98    7.90      1.03        8.13 0.0000460 0.0230 UP ptrap_volcano(   res_unpaired,   title = "pb vs nb — POA [unpaired t-test]" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: complex designs — multiple brain regions, custom blocking variables, non-standard column naming — supply sample_df directly:
code: # Example: two brain regions, two treatments sample_df <- data.frame(   sample    = c("POA_nb1_IP", "POA_nb1_INPUT", "POA_pb1_IP", "POA_pb1_INPUT",                 "AMY_nb1_IP", "AMY_nb1_INPUT", "AMY_pb1_IP", "AMY_pb1_INPUT"),   region    = rep(c("POA", "AMY"), each = 4),   treatment = rep(c("nb", "nb", "pb", "pb"), 2),   block     = rep("1", 8),   fraction  = rep(c("IP", "INPUT", "IP", "INPUT"), 2) )  res_poa_pb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = my_counts,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = my_gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   region_col     = "region",   treatment_col  = "treatment",   block_col      = "block",   fraction_col   = "fraction",   test_method    = "paired.ttest" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: use pTRAPPING research, please cite: Rodríguez, C. (2024). pTRAPPING: Suite Functions Analyses PhosphoTRAP Data R. https://github.com/laurenoconnelllab/pTRAPPING paired t-test method (test_method = "paired.ttest"): Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47–59. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 unpaired t-test method (test_method = "unpaired.ttest"): Knight, Z.., Tan, K., Birsoy, K., Schmidt, S., Garrison, J.L., Wysocki, R.W., Emiliano, ., Ekstrand, M.., Bhatt, D.L. (2012). Molecular profiling activated neurons phosphorylated ribosome capture. Cell 151, 1126–1137. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2012.10.039

what: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Compares IP vs INPUT fractions specified treatment condition using one six statistical approaches (see test_method). sample_df gene_ids optional: function can derive sample metadata automatically column names counts_mat, gene identifiers first column. "LRT" "QLF" use edgeR's GLM framework multi-factor design (~ fraction + block), accounting paired animal structure via blocking variable. methods automatically apply edgeR's TMM (trimmed mean M values) normalization via normLibSizes(), normalises effective library sizes (counts directly) use GLM. norm.method automatically forced "none" methods — CPM RPKM values, needed, computed TMM-adjusted effective library sizes. Recommended 4 replicates raw count data. "deseq" uses DESeq2 pipeline multi-factor design (~ block + fraction; fraction last results() auto-tests ). DESeq2 applies median ratios normalization internally via DESeq(), norm.method automatically forced "none". Recommended prefer negative-binomial shrinkage estimators DESeq2 edgeR. "voom" uses limma-voom pipeline paired design (~ fraction + block) empirical Bayes moderation. Voom internally transforms counts log2-CPM (using TMM-adjusted library sizes) computes precision weights mean-variance trend. transformation integral voom, norm.method automatically forced "none". "paired.ttest" runs per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT values across experimental repeats, following Tan et al. (2016) Cell 167, 47-59 doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 : "p value gene calculated paired t-test input immunoprecipitated RPKM values three experimental repeats." count scale test set norm.method (Tan et al. used "RPKM"; four norm.method options valid). logFC reported \(\log_2(\bar{\mathrm{IP}} / \bar{\mathrm{INPUT}})\). Recommended PhosphoTRAP experiments 3 replicates, GLM-based dispersion estimates unreliable. "unpaired.ttest" compares fold enrichments (IP/INPUT) two treatment groups using Welch (unpaired) t-test per-animal log2(FE) values. Use test whether ribosomal association differs conditions. Requires exactly two treatments; use treatment_name control_name specify . four norm.method options valid.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat,   sample_df = NULL,   gene_ids = NULL,   region_name = NULL,   treatment_name = NULL,   control_name = NULL,   sample_col = "sample",   fraction_col = "fraction",   block_col = "tube",   region_col = NULL,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   ip_level = "IP",   input_level = "INPUT",   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   test_method = c("LRT", "QLF", "paired.ttest", "unpaired.ttest", "voom", "deseq"),   norm.method = c("none", "CPM", "RPKM", "mratios"),   gene.length = NULL,   prior.count = 1,   return_long = FALSE,   ngenes.out = 20,   kable.out = FALSE )

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: counts_mat counts matrix one two formats: Matrix — numeric, genes x samples; column names sample IDs; gene IDs rownames supplied via gene_ids. Data frame / tibble — first column character vector gene IDs; remaining columns numeric counts sample names column names. sample_df = NULL, column names must follow naming convention described Automatic column-name parsing section. sample_df Optional data frame sample metadata. NULL (default), metadata parsed automatically column names counts_mat. provided, arguments sample_col, fraction_col, block_col, region_col, treatment_col specify columns use (falling back defaults names match). gene_ids Optional character vector gene identifiers corresponding rows counts_mat. NULL (default), gene IDs extracted first column counts_mat (data frame) rownames(counts_mat) (matrix). region_name brain region subset analyze (e.g., "POA"). used region_col NULL. Can NULL region_col = NULL (default) data contain single region. treatment_name treatment condition whose samples subsetted IP vs INPUT comparison (e.g., "pb" analyse pair-bonded samples). "unpaired.ttest", numerator condition differential fold enrichment (DFE = mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control). NULL (default), value derived automatically sample_df (parsed column names sample_df = NULL): single treatment found function proceeds silently; multiple treatments found error asks user specify one analyse. "unpaired.ttest" exactly two treatments treatment_name control_name NULL, assignments made alphabetically (first = control, second = treatment) message. control_name reference / control treatment used "unpaired.ttest". Serves denominator computing differential fold enrichment (DFE = mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control). NULL (default), function auto-detects control non-treatment_name group exactly two treatments present. sample_col Name column sample_df whose values match column names counts_mat. Ignored sample_df = NULL (auto-set internally). Default "sample". fraction_col Name column sample_df distinguishes IP INPUT fractions. Default "fraction". block_col Name column sample_df used blocking / pairing variable (e.g., individual animal tube). "LRT" / "QLF", "deseq", "voom" enters design matrix; "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest" aligns animal's IP INPUT. Default "tube". region_col Name column sample_df containing brain region labels. Set NULL (default) skip region filtering — recommended data already contain one brain region. set counts_mat contains multiple regions want analyze one time. Default NULL. treatment_col Name column sample_df containing treatment labels. Default "Treatment". ip_level value fraction_col identifies IP fraction. Default "IP". input_level value fraction_col identifies INPUT fraction (reference level). Default "INPUT". lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR (adjusted p-value) allowed classify gene differentially expressed. Default 0.05. test_method Statistical method use. One : "LRT" — edgeR likelihood ratio test via glmFit + glmLRT (default). TMM normalization applied automatically; norm.method forced "none". Suitable >= 4 replicates raw count data. "QLF" — edgeR quasi-likelihood F-test via glmQLFit + glmQLFTest. TMM normalization applied automatically; norm.method forced "none". conservative LRT. "deseq" — DESeq2 pipeline multi-factor design (~ block + fraction). Median ratios normalization applied automatically DESeq(); norm.method forced "none". Results obtained via results(dds, name = "fraction_IP_vs_INPUT"). "voom" — limma-voom pipeline paired design (~ fraction + block) empirical Bayes moderation via lmFit + eBayes + topTable. Log2-CPM + voom precision weights applied automatically; norm.method forced "none". "paired.ttest" — per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT values across replicates, following Tan et al. (2016). count scale set norm.method (Tan et al. used "RPKM"; four options valid). Best suited n = 3 replicates. P-values adjusted BH. "unpaired.ttest" — per-gene Welch unpaired t-test comparing log2(FE) values two treatment groups. count scale set norm.method (four options valid). Requires exactly two treatments; use treatment_name control_name specify groups. norm.method Normalization method applied counts t-test ("paired.ttest" / "unpaired.ttest" ). Ignored automatically set "none" "LRT", "QLF", "deseq", "voom", apply normalization internally (message shown supply non-"none" value). One : "none" (default) — uses TMM-adjusted raw counts stored DGEList (.e., count matrix rescaled, effective library sizes reflect TMM factors). Note: TMM normalises effective library sizes, counts ; CPM RPKM values derived TMM-adjusted sizes. "CPM" — counts per million, computed TMM-adjusted effective library sizes via edgeR::cpm(). Recommended comparisons replicates sample group. "RPKM" — reads per kilobase per million (CPM divided gene length kilobases), via edgeR::rpkm(). Requires gene.length. Recommended within-sample gene--gene comparisons (used Tan et al. 2016). suitable -sample comparisons. "mratios" — DESeq2 median ratios normalization via estimateSizeFactors() followed counts(dds, normalized = TRUE). Produces normalized count values suitable -sample comparisons. Recommended want DESeq2-style normalization t-test without running full DESeq2 pipeline. gene.length Named numeric vector gene lengths base pairs (names must match gene IDs). Required norm.method = "RPKM"; ignored otherwise. Default NULL. prior.count small count added IP INPUT values computing log2 ratios (FE = (IP + prior.count) / (INPUT + prior.count)), avoid log(0). Default 1. Set 0 values already normalised guaranteed positive. Used "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest". return_long Logical. used test_method "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest". TRUE, returns named list $results (DE tibble) $long_data (per-gene, per-animal table used test). Default FALSE. ngenes.Number top genes (sorted p-value) include output kable.= TRUE. Default 20. kable.Logical. TRUE, returns kableExtra HTML table top ngenes.genes instead full tibble. Default FALSE.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: kable.= FALSE (default), tibble one row per gene sorted p-value. Columns depend test_method: "LRT" / "QLF": Gene, logFC, logCPM, LR F, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed ("", "", ""). "deseq": Gene, baseMean, logFC (log2FoldChange DESeq2), lfcSE, stat, PValue, FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted p-value results()), treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. Genes NA p-values (outliers low counts flagged DESeq2) retained sorted bottom. "paired.ttest": Gene, logFC (\(\log_2(\bar{\mathrm{IP}} / \bar{\mathrm{INPUT}})\)), t_statistic (paired t-test IP \(-\) INPUT differences), PValue, FE_<block> columns (one per animal giving linear IP/INPUT fold enrichment animal), FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. return_long = TRUE, returns named list: $results (tibble ) $long_data (per-gene, per-animal tibble columns Gene, block column, ip_count, input_count, FE). "unpaired.ttest": Gene, logFC (log2(mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control)), diff_FE, mean_FE_<treatment>, mean_FE_<control>, t_statistic, df (Welch degrees freedom), PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. return_long = TRUE, returns named list: $results $long_data (per-gene, per-animal, per-treatment tibble columns Gene, block column, treatment column, FE, log2_FE). "voom": Gene, logFC, AveExpr, t, B, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. kable.= TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes.genes.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Perform differential expression analysis PhosphoTRAP data using edgeR, paired t-test, unpaired t-test, voom/limma

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: sample_df = NULL, column names counts_mat (excluding first, gene-ID column) parsed build sample metadata automatically. column name must encode three pieces information, order combination separators (_, -, ., space) separator : Treatment One letters identifying experimental group. Replicate number digit identifying biological replicate. Fraction ip_level input_level (case-insensitive); "" also accepted short alias INPUT fraction. Valid column name examples (default ip_level = "IP", input_level = "INPUT"):

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47-59. doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 Love, M.., Huber, W., Anders, S. (2014). Moderated estimation fold change dispersion RNA-seq data DESeq2. Genome Biology 15, 550. doi:10.1186/s13059-014-0550-8

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## ---- Option A: simplest call — auto-parse from column names --------------- # counts_mat is a data frame where: #   col 1      = gene IDs #   col 2+     = samples named like "b1input", "b1ip", "nb2input", etc. counts <- read.table("counts.txt", header = TRUE)  # single treatment in the matrix — treatment_name auto-detected res <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, test_method = "paired.ttest")  # multiple treatments — specify which one to analyze res_b <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, treatment_name = "b")  ## ---- Option B: provide sample_df explicitly (original workflow) ----------- res_lrt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # quasi-likelihood F-test res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # paired t-test — also return long-format paired table res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) res_pt$results    # DE tibble res_pt$long_data  # per-gene, per-animal pairs  # unpaired t-test — compare fold enrichment between two treatments res_upt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   treatment_name = "pb",   control_name   = "nb",   test_method    = "unpaired.ttest" )  # voom/limma pipeline res_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "voom" )  # paired t-test with CPM normalization res_cpm <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   norm.method    = "CPM" ) } # }

what: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: Takes single tibble produced ptrap_de() returns classic volcano plot (logFC x-axis, -log10(FDR) y-axis). Non-significant genes shown grey; genes classified "" "" diffexpressed column highlighted distinct fill colours labelled gene identifier. Dashed threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold (vertical) -log10(fdr_threshold) (horizontal).

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: ptrap_volcano(   de_result,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: de_result tibble returned ptrap_de(), containing minimum columns Gene (gene_col), logFC, FDR, diffexpressed. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change used draw vertical threshold lines. match value used ptrap_de(). Default 1. fdr_threshold FDR cutoff used draw horizontal threshold line (plotted -log10(fdr_threshold)). match value used ptrap_de(). Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label. Used build default plot title. Default "Treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used build default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping "" "" colours. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used ("#D55E00" "", "#0072B2" ""). point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity highlighted (significant) points. Default 0.7. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels suppressed. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), title auto-generated brain region treatment labels found de_result.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # Default plot ptrap_volcano(res)  # Custom thresholds and colours ptrap_volcano(res,               lfc_threshold = 0.5,               fdr_threshold = 0.1,               colors        = c("UP" = "#E69F00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9")) } # }

what: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: Takes two tibbles produced ptrap_de() similar — one per treatment condition, brain region — joins gene, classifies gene according differential expression status condition, returns scatter plot logFC\(_{\text{treatment 1}}\) vs logFC\(_{\text{treatment 2}}\). Significant genes highlighted labelled; threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold axes.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: ptrap_volcano2(   de_result_1,   de_result_2,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "Treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: de_result_1 tibble returned ptrap_de() first treatment condition (plotted y-axis). de_result_2 tibble returned ptrap_de() second treatment condition (plotted x-axis). lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change used define significance. Must match (stricter ) threshold used ptrap_de(). Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR used define significance. Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers result tibbles. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label result tibbles. Used auto-generate axis labels DE class names. Default "Treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used set default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping DE class colour. Names must "DE ", "DE <t1>", "DE <t2>", <t1> / <t2> treatment names found data. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used. point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity coloured (significant) points. Default 0.7. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels allowed hidden. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), brain region name extracted de_result_1 used.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res_pb  <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "pb") res_sol <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "sol")  # Default plot — treatment 1 on y-axis, treatment 2 on x-axis ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol)  # Custom thresholds, title and colours ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol,                lfc_threshold = 0.5,                fdr_threshold = 0.1,                title         = "POA: pb vs sol",                colors        = c("DE in both"  = "#D55E00",                                  "DE only pb"  = "#0072B2",                                  "DE only sol" = "#49a15f")) } # }
